biography.identity: Master Chief Petty Officer Dain A. Franklyn, United States Navy, is a native of Ocho Rios, Jamaica. He graduated from York Castle High School in Browns Town, Jamaica, in 1994. He later earned an Associate and Bachelor's Degree in Applied Sciences from Wayland Baptist University in 2017. He is currently pursuing a Masters degree in Computer Information Systems from the Florida Institute of Technology and expects to complete in July 2026.
biography.enlistment: He enlisted in the U.S. Navy in September 2005 and reported to Recruit Training Command, Great Lakes, Illinois, for basic training. Upon completion, he reported to USS Germantown (LSD 42) as a deck seaman from January 2006 to June 2007. He rated as an Aviation Machinist's Mate (AD) in May 2007.
biography.operationalAssignments: His operational assignments include Helicopter Anti-Submarine Warfare Squadron TEN (HS-10) from September 2007 to September 2009, where he attained the rank of Petty Officer Second Class; Helicopter Maritime Strike Squadron THREE SEVEN (HSM-37) from September 2009 to November 2017, where he advanced to Chief Petty Officer; Helicopter Maritime Strike Squadron FOUR ZERO (HSM-40) from November 2017 to November 2020, where he was promoted to Senior Chief Petty Officer; and USS Harry S. Truman (CVN 75) from November 2020 to August 2024, where he attained the rank of Master Chief Petty Officer.
biography.otherAssignments: He then served aboard Pre-Commissioning Unit John F. Kennedy (CVN 79) from August 2024 to May 2025. In May 2025, he reported to Commander, Naval Air Forces Atlantic (CNAL), where he currently serves as Assistant Project Officer for Maintenance Management Information Systems.
biography.unitAwards: Master Chief Franklyn is a graduate of the U.S. Navy Senior Enlisted Academy. He has been part of four Battle "E" award-winning units and one unit that received the Coast Guard Meritorious Unit Commendation with Operational Device ("O").
biography.personalAwards: His personal awards include four Navy and Marine Corps Commendation Medals, six Navy and Marine Corps Achievement Medals, the Military Outstanding Volunteer Service Medal, Global War on Terrorism Expeditionary Medal, Global War on Terrorism Service Medal, and six Sea Service Deployment Ribbons.
